metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4669
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1E-F: Authors utilized a pHluorin reporter (super-ecliptic pHluorin: SEP) fused to the luminal C-terminal portions of Stx7 to image localization of Stx7 in cultured hippocampal neurons. Stx7-SEP exhibited unique behavior, i.e., stimulation at 10 Hz failed to elicit a clear fluorescence increase, while 40 Hz stimulation caused a robust increase up to ~40%.

Figure 2: Exocytosis of Stx7-SEP vesicles requires HFS. Authors continuously monitored changes in SEP fluorescence at individual boutons with various stimulation frequencies ranging from 5 to 40 Hz at 5 min intervals. Unlike SypHy, which reliably exhibited a robust exocytotic fluorescence increase irrespective of stimulation frequency, Stx7-SEP hardly responded during 5 Hz or 10 Hz stimulation, while it showed robust fluorescence increases at 20 and 40 Hz in the same boutons.

Authors conclude; "Our results collectively indicate that endosomal fusion supported by Stx7, presumably together with its cognate SNAREs, is responsible for generating a functionally distinct recycling pool of SVs". Experimental description: - Primary hippocampal cultures were prepared from embryonic day 16 ICR mice
- pHluorin reporter (super-ecliptic pHluorin: SEP) fused to the luminal C-terminal portions of Stx7
- rabbit polyclonal anti-Stx7 antibody (BethylLaboratories; A304-512A),

'"To generate recombinant proteins as standards for quantification analysis, Stx7-NTD (a.a. 1-149) and Syb2-ΔTMD (a.a. 1-94) (accession no. NM_009497.3) were amplified by PCR, and cloned into the BamHI site and SalI site of pGEX6P-1. Lentiviral vectors carrying mCherry and either scrambled shRNA (CCTAAGGTTAAGTCGCCCTCG), mouse Stx7 shRNA-#1 (CGATATGATTGACAGCATAGA), or mouse Stx7 shRNA-#2; (GAAGCTGATATTATGGACATT) were obtained from VectorBuilder Biotechnology Co. Ltd."
